message:
feat(mvcc): Silo-style epoch group commit primitive (IMPL-16 / AG-5A)

Scaffold the Silo (Tu et al., OSDI 2013) epoch group commit controller
in `fsqlite-mvcc::silo_epoch`. MT-bench on current main degrades from
43k -> 30k writes/sec going 4->8 threads because every transaction pays
the per-txn WAL fsync. Group-committing within a bounded epoch window
amortizes that fsync across the bucket.

Components
- EpochGroupCommit: AtomicU64 epoch, parking_lot::Mutex<Vec<Pending>>,
  caller-supplied Box<dyn Fn()> flush callback, background advancer
  thread holding a Weak<Self> so it does not keep the controller alive.
- CommitWaiter: (txn_id, Arc<AtomicBool> ready, epoch_at_submit,
  Arc<WaiterNotifier>) where the notifier is a parking_lot Mutex/Condvar
  pair the advancer uses to wake the caller.
- submit / advance_epoch / wait_for_commit / wait_for_commit_timeout.
- advance_epoch takes the pending lock, steals the Vec, bumps the epoch
  *under the lock* (so concurrent submitters see the new epoch on their
  next load), invokes the flush callback, then marks every stolen waiter
  ready + notify_all. Flush runs before ready=true so wakeup implies
  durability.
- Drop: sets shutdown, drains stragglers so no one blocks forever,
  joins the advancer (bounded by one epoch window).

Tests (parking_lot deadlock-free, 10s epoch in tests so the background
thread stays idle):
1. advance_epoch_resolves_all_pending: 100 submits + 1 advance_epoch
   -> all ready.
2. waiter_blocks_without_advance: submit, wait_for_commit_timeout(100us)
   -> not resolved.
3. multi_threaded_submit_and_advance: 4 submitters x 200 txns + 1
   advancer thread -> every waiter resolved.

Not yet wired into real commit paths; follow-up will replace the
no-op FlushFn with the actual WAL fsync and call submit() from the
commit routine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
